meta.title: Earley Grammar — Where We're At
meta.description: Catch-up dashboard: baseline position, plan hardening, S0-S5 implementation plan, promotion ladder, Qwen recovery position

props.title: Template-Compiled Earley Grammar
props.subtitle: Shovel-ready. Plan stress-tested against the live repo, every verified hole patched, prerequisite landed.

props.content: The grammar engine is the next milestone (M5) of the candidate pipeline. It compiles existing authority template rows into Earley productions and emits shadow Candidate evidence through the same PolicyChain seam everything else uses — no new parser system, no new authority path. The plan survived an adversarial audit today; what follows is exactly where things stand and what gets built next.

props.label: failing rows — only 8 in the 4 pilot grammar families

items.title: Shadow grammar lane proven (M0–M4 + S5–S7)
items.description: Typed Candidate evidence model, trace projection, deterministic validation, and a Phase-0 hand-parser GrammarCandidateProvider running shadow in production for heading / altitude / frequency / squawk. PolicyChain keeps it trace-only.

items.title: Plan + design + task docs approved
items.description: Arch loop ended APPROVED: one pipeline (SegmentCandidateProvider → Candidate → PolicyChain → candidate_trace/metrics), Earley under modules/intent/grammar/, Qwen fenced to recovery.

items.title: Typed-evidence cutover landed (26dbf3908)
items.description: Metadata fallback/hydration deleted; typed Candidate evidence is sole authority; provider seam moved to provider_ingress_validation.py; protected-key validators guard FMM/readback payloads. Verified: 222 focused tests, infra panel 42 pass, baseline-self-parity green.

items.title: Plan hardened against measured reality (f0bcb7a3e)
items.description: Adversarial audit found 10 holes (stale gates, an unsatisfiable S5 verifier, missing receipt step, scanner tolerance gap). All patched in plan + execution.yaml; lev task validate PASS; Qwen recovery audit recorded in UPGRADES.md Epic 8.

items.title: Execute S0 → S5
items.description: Capture the pre-baseline receipt from the now-clean tree, then build compiler → parser → semantics → CYK retirement → shadow evaluation. TDD inside each slice; every slice has its own verifier contract.

props.label: ARCHITECTURE
props.title: One spine, grammar is just another provider
props.subtitle: Grammar emits evidence. PolicyChain selects. Nothing else gets authority.

props.content:
flowchart LR
  T["authority templates
templates/intents/*.json"] --> C["template_compiler.py
rows to productions + provenance"]
  C --> IR["grammar_ir.py
immutable productions / slot bindings"]
  L["scanner.py / lexemes.py
slot-family lexeme registry"] --> E
  IR --> E["earley.py
predict / scan / complete"]
  E --> S["semantics.py
ParseResult to Candidate evidence
validation.contract_row_id = row id"]
  S --> G["engine.py
fail-closed factory"]
  G --> A["GrammarCandidateProvider
thin adapter, existing seam"]
  A --> PC["PolicyChain
mode: shadow, trust_tier 55"]
  PC --> TR["candidate_trace + candidate_metrics
promotion evidence"]
props.caption: Target landing zone: modules/intent/grammar/ — grammar emits evidence, PolicyChain selects

props.label: TODAY'S AUDIT
props.title: Holes found, all patched
props.subtitle: Each one verified against live code or live baseline trace data before patching.

rows.evidence: Trace projector never surfaces matched_patterns/data_refs; Phase-0 grammar rows carry empty contract_row_id
rows.patch: Slice 3 now requires validation.contract_row_id = template row id (the field the projector already projects)

rows.evidence: Real gate is MAX_POST_SPLIT_LOC=500; arbitration file is 357 LOC post-cutover
S5 verifier hard-fails without sha256/git-head/row-count metadata
rows.patch: Plan now points at the execution.yaml S0 fitness command

rows.evidence: B1 ASR: 'chain to MY frequency 124.5' — exact-literal scan re-misses what regex misses
rows.patch: Slice 2 requires bounded substitution on non-slot literals only; slot values never fuzzy-match

rows.evidence: Pre-baseline receipt from a dirty tree proves nothing
rows.patch: Typed cutover committed first (26dbf3908); plan states the ordering

rows.evidence: SegmentCandidateProvider lives in provider_ingress_validation.py, not policy_chain/protocol.py
Pilot families = only 8 of 77 failing rows (~10% of failure mass)
rows.patch: Measured Position + promotion ladder sections added to plan

rows.evidence: Live shadow emitted 171.219 (out of band) on adiz phase_06
rows.patch: Confirmed load-bearing; dynamic lexicon stays a promotion blocker for frequency_change

rows.evidence: run_eval silently overwrote gitignored trace files
rows.patch: Now refuses without --overwrite; 18 POC tests green

props.label: THE PLAN
props.title: Implementation slices S0 → S5
props.subtitle: TDD inside each slice: red tests first, verifier contract per slice (pytest + static proof scans). Grammar stays mode: shadow throughout — zero selected-winner drift is the exit criterion.

props.title: S0
props.content: Docs aligned + pre-baseline receipt & meta from clean tree

rows.contract: Captured BEFORE any runtime code edit, from the clean post-cutover tree
rows.proof: Receipt exists; sha256 + git head + row count recorded

rows.contract: Compile heading/altitude/frequency/squawk authority rows via IntentTemplateAuthority; preserve row_id + source provenance; immutable cached grammar
predict/scan/complete, top-k deterministic parses; lexeme resolvers by slot family; bounded substitution for non-slot literals ONLY — no silent repair of slot values
ParseResult to typed Candidate evidence; validation.contract_row_id = template row id (makes S5 gate satisfiable); fail-closed engine; no PolicyChain._ access; arbitration file <= 500 LOC
CYK is test-only stub today (3 rules); migrate useful behavior cases into Earley tests first
rows.proof: negative rg scan + Earley suites green

rows.contract: post vs pre baseline: selected behavior + winner source/id identical after dropping trace/metric/timing; grammar in trace for all 4 families with row/rule provenance; NO grammar candidate selected while shadow

props.label: AFTER S5
props.title: Promotion ladder — driven by measured failures, not convenience
props.subtitle: The pilot proves safety; the yield lives further up the ladder.

props.title: 🎯 Rung 1 — Pilot families (8 failing rows)
props.content: heading / altitude / frequency / squawk. Shadow grammar is ALREADY exactly-correct on 4 of 5 failing A1 rows — e.g. raw misparsed 'contact ground 121.9' as taxi_instruction while grammar shadow held frequency_change {ground, 121.9}. These are the first per-family promotion reviews. frequency_change stays blocked on dynamic lexicon (live 171.219 false positive).

props.title: 🧩 Rung 2 — Compound (15 failing rows)
props.content: The single biggest failure cluster: whole-turn utterances misread as one intent ('expected compound, got sequencing'). This is exactly where a chart parser over clause sequences beats per-segment regex — the Earley chart can parse an utterance as a sequence of command clauses. Highest-yield expansion, hardest grammar.

props.title: 📡 Rung 3 — Long tail
props.content: informational (6), traffic_advisory (3), verification_request (3), comm_request / route_amendment / position_report / direct_to (2 each). Each expansion is its own shadow-then-promote slice through the same Candidate/PolicyChain seam. Template compilation means new families are mostly data, not code.

props.title: A family flips shadow → active only when
props.content: Passing rows preserved · failing rows fixed BY the grammar candidate (trace-attributed) · required slots + bounds validation pass · candidate trace names the winning row/rule · baseline-self-parity green · just baseline --json --no-write proves candidate-attributed improvement · policy change is per-family and reversible in one overlay edit.

props.title: Hard boundaries that do not move
props.content: Candidate is the only evidence object crossing the provider seam · PolicyChain is the only global selector · grammar stays mode: shadow through this entire plan · no parser evidence leaks into ParsedATCCommand / SegmentAuthorityRecord · no new dependencies · DeBERTa stays disabled · Qwen parser promotion is out of scope.

props.label: RECOVERY LANE
props.title: Qwen recovery — more built than expected, four real gaps
props.subtitle: Audited today; verified position recorded in UPGRADES.md Epic 8. Qwen is gateway-owned recovery AFTER grammar + dynamic lexicon are exhausted — never parser authority.

props.title: ✅ Already built and working
props.content: Recovery decision contract (modules/recovery/) with strict JSON schema + abstention. Gateway QwenRecoveryClient + suspicion triggers (no_intent, low_confidence, fmm_rejected, readback_unrenderable, suspect_signal, advisory_backfill). Model server with mock/mlx/cuda backends and warmup-gated /ready. Full NAAC_QWEN_RECOVERY_* flag surface. POC eval harness verified (dry-run + 18 unit tests).

props.title: ⚠️ Gaps before any promotion talk
props.content: 1) Apply mode is HARDCODED off — prod router forces advisory_only and ignores gate.qwen_recovery_mode; moving past telemetry is a code change + its own gate. 2) The a1_a2_readback_repair eval (25 baseline-failing rows) has never run against a real model. 3) parser_disagreement / critical_slot_failure triggers don't exist — yet baseline already counts both (771 / 469 rows). 4) Thor deploy disabled; quantize script still references Qwen2.5 while configs want Qwen3-4B.

**Grammar lane:** run the S0 fitness command from execution.yaml to capture the pre-baseline receipt from the clean tree, then S1 template compiler with red tests first. Each slice lands with its verifier green before the next starts; `just baseline-self-parity` is the per-tick determinism gate.

**Qwen lane (parallel, independent):** run a1_a2_readback_repair against mlx locally and version the traces → add the two missing triggers behind shadow telemetry → wire recovery scoring into a periodic `just baseline_recovery` lane → only then write the apply_readback promotion gate.

Everything is committed and pushed: typed cutover 26dbf3908, plan hardening f0bcb7a3e, workstream record a685fc9d5. The plan docs are the single source of truth: `.lev/pm/plans/plan-impl-template-compiled-earley-grammar.md` and `.lev/pm/tasks/template-compiled-earley-grammar/execution.yaml`.
